text.title: ADR Staleness Audit Output Contract
text.summary: Defines the audit report and per-ADR transition schemas.

forbidden.pattern: ADR with decision != "accepted" but no evidence_links
ADR text edited or deleted during audit (only status / metadata may change)
"deprecated" decision without a follow_up_action explaining why no successor
"superseded" decision without a linked superseding_adr id (even if draft)
aggregate_score outside [0,1] OR not matching the signal mapping
audit run more than 14 days outside the quarterly window (signals stale)

allowed-transformations.transformation: Signal 3 may be marked "N/A" when reversibility tagging is not in use; aggregate_score then divides by 3 instead of 4.
"re_open" status may temporarily hold an ADR for 30 days max; if no decision after 30 days, force to deprecated or write a superseder.
For ADRs flagged by the decay-detector-agent with high confidence, the manual code-reality signal may inherit the agent's score with a citation.

list.item: every audited ADR has all 4 signals scored (with 0.5 / N/A where appropriate)
evidence_links present for any decision != accepted
follow_up_action + deadline for re_open / superseded / deprecated
superseding_adrs_drafted (at least skeletons) for every "superseded" decision
